mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate spikes above 9.0 percent, it signals a rapid deterioration in consumer spending capacity, triggering a sell-off in cyclical equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in housing starts and a decline in real consumer credit availability indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight capacity utilization, a sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects pricing power and margin expansion, which positively drives S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during recessionary periods defined by NBER.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth expands the earnings base for aggregate corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window as momentum traders front-run the data.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between GDP growth acceleration and SPX forward returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns are flat across all deciles of GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to discount long-duration growth stocks, causing a relative valuation compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite rates exceeding 0.25 percent, or the forward return differential between pre- and post-threshold periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment grows while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicates a labor force participation surge that dilutes wage pressure, signaling weak underlying demand and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-factor models, or forward returns show no significant difference between divergence and convergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sustained rise in real consumer credit availability fuels nominal spending that outpaces earnings growth, eventually triggering a valuation reset and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when RSAFS rises alongside CPIAUCSL above 3.0 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below chance levels in backtesting.
